hypothesis: A swarm architecture in which multiple parallel LLM sub-agents independently explore the same atomic layer processing reactor simulation with randomized initial conditions and stochastically varied model temperatures, followed by a meta-agent that synthesizes their divergent experimental trajectories, will discover a significantly higher fraction of the total available chemical interaction space within equivalent total experimental time compared to any single-agent exploration, with the improvement scaling superlinearly with swarm size up to a saturation point determined by the dimensionality of the input-output space.
